steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both texts diagnose a shared problem in modernist scholarship: an overly narrow focus that limits what the field can see. The correct choice must identify a logical conclusion both authors would agree follows from that shared diagnosis.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Without a major shift in focus, the vision that it presents of fiction written in the period will continue to be unnecessarily limited.: Both texts argue that the current narrow focus obscures important patterns. A continued narrow focus would keep those patterns hidden, making the field's picture of modernism incomplete. Both authors support this. ✓
  • Choice B — It must widen its focus to include aspects of modernist fiction beyond style, a productive but overrepresented site of inquiry.: Describes the wrong corrective; neither text calls for what B proposes. ✗
  • Choice C — Its primary methods for analyzing fiction written in the period are growing obsolete as computer technology advances.: Neither text makes or implies this claim. ✗
  • Choice D — Instead of engaging in unproductive debates, it should work to rehabilitate the reputations of neglected modernist works.: Addresses recovery of specific works rather than the systemic problem of scholarly focus both texts identify. ✗
That is the correct answer.
